name: O-1
tagline: For individuals with extraordinary achievement in their field — no lottery, no employer cap
description: The O-1 visa is for individuals who have demonstrated extraordinary ability in the sciences, arts, education, business, or athletics (O-1A), or extraordinary achievement in the motion picture or television industry (O-1B). Unlike H-1B, there is no annual cap or lottery — petitions can be filed at any time of year and are not subject to numerical limitations.

requirements: Must demonstrate sustained national or international acclaim in your field
For O-1A (sciences, business, education, athletics): must meet at least 3 of 8 USCIS criteria, or demonstrate a major internationally recognized award (e.g., Nobel Prize, Olympic medal)
A U.S. employer or agent must file the petition on your behalf
The position must require someone of extraordinary ability
Must have a job offer, consulting contract, or series of engagements
benefits: No annual cap or lottery — file any time of year
Initial period of up to 3 years, extendable in 1-year increments with no limit
Allows dual intent — you can pursue a green card (EB-1A) while on O-1
Spouse and children receive O-3 dependent status (not authorized to work)
Can work for multiple employers simultaneously if filed properly
Strong pathway to EB-1A green card for extraordinary ability individuals

steps.title: Evidence gathering
steps.description: Compile evidence of extraordinary ability: awards, publications, high salary, critical roles, judging peers, press coverage, etc.

steps.title: Advisory opinion
steps.description: For most O-1 cases, a written advisory opinion from a relevant peer group or labor organization is required.

steps.title: Petition filing
steps.description: Employer or agent files Form I-129 with O supplement, evidence package, and advisory opinion. Premium processing available.

steps.title: Visa stamp (if abroad)
steps.description: If outside the U.S., attend consular interview. If inside the U.S., change of status is included in the petition.

processing.timeline: 2–4 months standard; 15 business days with premium processing ($2,805)
processing.cost: Government fees: $460–$3,265 depending on options. Premium processing: $2,805. Attorney fees additional.
documents: Evidence meeting at least 3 of the 8 O-1A criteria

faqs.question: What are the 8 criteria for O-1A extraordinary ability?
faqs.answer: USCIS evaluates O-1A petitions using 8 criteria. You must meet at least 3: (1) receipt of nationally or internationally recognized prizes or awards, (2) membership in associations requiring outstanding achievement, (3) published material about you in professional publications or major media, (4) participation as a judge of others' work, (5) original contributions of major significance, (6) authorship of scholarly articles, (7) critical role at distinguished organizations, and (8) high salary relative to peers. Meeting more criteria strengthens the petition.

faqs.question: Can a startup founder self-petition for O-1?
faqs.answer: Not directly — O-1 requires a U.S. employer or agent to file the petition. However, founders can use an immigration agent (a third-party entity authorized to act as petitioner) or structure a separate U.S. entity to be the petitioner. We help founders navigate this structure regularly. The O-1A is one of the best visa options for startup founders because it has no lottery and no employer dependency once the agent structure is established.

faqs.question: How does O-1A relate to EB-1A green card?
faqs.answer: O-1A and EB-1A use very similar standards — both require extraordinary ability. An approved O-1A petition is strong evidence for an EB-1A green card application, though the two processes are separate. Many clients pursue O-1A first while building their EB-1A case. The EB-1A allows self-petition (no employer required) and leads to a green card.

faqs.question: I'm a tech professional — do I qualify for O-1A?
faqs.answer: Many tech professionals qualify for O-1A, especially those who have contributed to open source projects, published research, been cited by peers, spoken at major conferences, received industry awards, or held critical roles at recognized tech companies. The standard requires more than just being good at your job — you need to demonstrate national or international recognition. We offer case evaluations to help you understand your eligibility.

faqs.question: Can I change jobs on O-1 status?
faqs.answer: Unlike H-1B, O-1 portability is more restricted. If you change employers, the new employer must file a new O-1 petition before you begin working. Premium processing ensures a quick turnaround. If using an agent, the agent can file a new petition to cover the new engagement.
